meta.title: Global Job Market 2023-24
meta.primarySource: ILO World Employment and Social Outlook: Trends 2024, International Labour Organization, Geneva
meta.notes: Sector shares from ILO ILOSTAT sectoral employment database 2023. Salaries are nominal USD/month global medians from ILO Global Wage Report 2024/25. AI exposure based on Oxford Martin School (Frey & Osborne 2017 update) and McKinsey Global Institute 2023 methodology.

sectors.name: Agriculture & Food Systems
sources: ILO ILOSTAT Sectoral Employment 2023 — Agriculture share 26.7% of global workforce
FAO The State of Food and Agriculture 2023
World Bank World Development Report 2024

occupations.name: Crop Farmers & Cultivators
occupations.description: Smallholder and commercial crop farmers globally. ~70% in Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa. Mechanisation and climate stress driving structural decline.
sources: FAO The State of Food and Agriculture 2023 — 500M smallholder farming households
ILO ILOSTAT agricultural employment by occupation 2023
World Bank Agriculture and Food Global Practice 2024

occupations.name: Livestock & Dairy Workers
occupations.description: Cattle, poultry, dairy farm workers. Growing demand from rising protein consumption in emerging markets.
sources: FAO Livestock & Livelihoods 2023
ILO Global Employment Trends in Agriculture 2023

occupations.name: Fisheries & Aquaculture
occupations.description: 600M people depend on fisheries for livelihoods. Aquaculture is world's fastest-growing food sector.
sources: FAO The State of World Fisheries and Aquaculture 2024 — 600M livelihoods, 60M directly employed

occupations.name: Agricultural Wage Labour
occupations.description: Seasonal and permanent wage labourers on farms globally. Most vulnerable to automation and climate displacement.
sources: ILO Global Wage Report 2024/25 — agricultural wages by region
ILO ILOSTAT casual agricultural employment 2023

sectors.name: Manufacturing & Industry
sources: ILO ILOSTAT Sectoral Employment 2023 — Manufacturing 16.1% global share
UNIDO World Manufacturing Production 2024
ILO Global Supply Chain Report 2023

occupations.name: Factory & Assembly Line Workers
occupations.description: Assembly, packaging, production line workers globally. Robot adoption accelerating in high-wage economies; shifting to Vietnam, Bangladesh, Ethiopia.
sources: ILO Global Supply Chains and Decent Work 2023
IFR World Robotics Report 2024 — 3.5M industrial robots installed
UNIDO World Manufacturing Production Statistics 2024

occupations.name: Garment & Textile Workers
occupations.description: 75M workers globally; 80% women. Concentrated in Bangladesh, China, Vietnam, India, Ethiopia. Brands shifting supply chains post-pandemic.
sources: ILO Textiles, Clothing, Leather and Footwear Sector 2023
ILO Better Work Programme Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Food & Beverage Processing
occupations.description: Largest manufacturing sub-sector by employment. Packaged food, beverages, dairy. Resilient to downturns.
sources: ILO Food & Drink Industries Global Employment 2023
FAO Agri-food Systems Employment Report 2024

occupations.name: Automobile & Engineering
occupations.description: 8M direct auto workers + 50M in supply chain. EV transition disrupting powertrain jobs but creating battery/software roles.
sources: ILO Future of Work in the Automotive Sector 2023
OICA (International Org of Motor Vehicle Manufacturers) Employment Data 2024

occupations.name: Electronics & Semiconductors
occupations.description: Chips, consumer electronics, components. Geopolitical reshoring (CHIPS Act, EU Chips Act) driving new plant construction in US, Europe, India.
sources: SIA (Semiconductor Industry Association) Global Employment Data 2024
SEMI Global Semiconductor Equipment Market Statistics 2024

occupations.name: Pharma & Chemical Workers
occupations.description: Life sciences and specialty chemicals. Post-COVID reshoring of API manufacturing from China to India, Europe, US.
sources: IFPMA (International Federation of Pharmaceutical Manufacturers) Data 2024
ICCA (International Council of Chemical Associations) Employment Report 2023

sectors.name: Trade, Wholesale & Retail
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Trade sector 15.1% of global workforce
World Trade Organization Global Trade Outlook 2024
UNCTAD Digital Economy Report 2024

occupations.name: Retail Store Workers
occupations.description: 2nd largest global employer after agriculture. Amazon, Walmart automation threatening checkout and warehouse roles in high-wage markets.
sources: ILO Commerce Sector Employment 2023
WTO Annual Report 2024 — global retail employment

occupations.name: E-commerce & Digital Retail
occupations.description: Global e-commerce $5.8Tr in 2023. Created 50M+ direct jobs; fastest growing segment in retail globally.
sources: UNCTAD Digital Economy Report 2024 — e-commerce $5.8Tr
eMarketer Global E-Commerce Forecast 2024

occupations.name: Wholesale & Distribution
occupations.description: B2B trade intermediaries, FMCG distributors, commodity traders connecting manufacturers to retailers globally.
sources: ILO ILOSTAT wholesale trade employment 2023
World Bank Enterprise Survey 2024

occupations.name: Informal & Street Traders
occupations.description: Street vendors, market traders primarily in Africa, Asia, Latin America. ILO estimates 60% of global employment is informal.
sources: ILO Women and Men in the Informal Economy 2023 — 60% informal workforce globally
World Bank Informal Economy Report 2024

sectors.name: Construction
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Construction 6% of global workforce
Oxford Economics Global Construction 2030 Report
World Bank Infrastructure Report 2024

occupations.name: General Construction Workers
occupations.description: Masons, carpenters, steel fixers, site labourers. Demand driven by $15Tr global infrastructure gap (G20 Global Infrastructure Hub).
sources: ILO Construction Sector Employment 2023
G20 Global Infrastructure Hub Investment Gap Report 2023

occupations.name: Civil & Structural Engineers
occupations.description: Infrastructure engineers globally. Chronic shortage in developing nations; AI-assisted BIM changing design workflows.
sources: FIDIC (International Federation of Consulting Engineers) Global Report 2024
World Bank Infrastructure Finance 2024

occupations.name: Electricians, Plumbers & Trades
occupations.description: Critical skilled trades for green building, solar installation, EV charging networks. Severe shortage in EU, US, Australia.
sources: ILO Skills for Green Jobs 2023
IRENA Renewable Energy and Jobs Annual Review 2024

occupations.name: Real Estate Agents & Developers
occupations.description: Property agents, developers, valuers. Global real estate market $326Tr (Savills 2023). Proptech AI disrupting agent roles.
sources: Savills Global Real Estate Market Report 2023 — $326Tr total value
NAR (National Assoc. of Realtors) International Data 2024

sectors.name: Transport & Logistics
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Transport 6% global workforce
ITF (International Transport Forum) Transport Outlook 2024
World Bank Logistics Performance Index 2023

occupations.name: Truck & HGV Drivers
occupations.description: Highest AI-exposure transport role. Autonomous trucking pilots (Waymo Via, Plus.ai, TuSimple) progressing in US, China, EU. Shortage of 3M drivers globally (IRU 2023).
sources: IRU (International Road Transport Union) Driver Shortage Report 2023 — 3M shortage
ILO Future of Work in Transport 2023

occupations.name: Ride-hail & Gig Delivery
occupations.description: Uber, Lyft, DiDi, Grab, Bolt drivers + DoorDash, Deliveroo gig workers. ILO estimates 100M+ platform workers globally.
sources: ILO World Employment and Social Outlook 2021 — platform workers 100M+
OECD Digital Platform Work Report 2024

occupations.name: Aviation (Pilots, Crew, Ground)
occupations.description: 4.5Bn passengers in 2023 (ICAO). Global pilot shortage of 80,000 by 2032 (Boeing). Fastest post-COVID recovery sector.
sources: ICAO Annual Report 2023 — 4.5Bn passengers
Boeing Pilot & Technician Outlook 2024-2043 — 800K new pilots needed

occupations.name: Maritime & Shipping
occupations.description: 80% of world trade moves by sea. 1.89M seafarers on merchant ships (BIMCO/ICS 2023). Port automation accelerating.
sources: BIMCO/ICS Seafarer Workforce Report 2023 — 1.89M seafarers
UNCTAD Review of Maritime Transport 2024

occupations.name: Logistics, Warehousing & Fulfilment
occupations.description: E-commerce boom driving warehouse expansion. Amazon alone employs 1.5M globally. Robotics (Kiva, Locus) automating picking.
sources: Global Logistics & Supply Chain Management Market Report 2024 — Mordor Intelligence
ILO E-Commerce and Logistics Employment 2023

sectors.name: Healthcare & Social Work
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Healthcare 3.1% of global workforce
WHO Global Health Workforce Statistics 2024
ILO Care Work and Care Jobs for the Future 2023

occupations.name: Doctors & Physicians
occupations.description: WHO estimates global shortage of 10M health workers by 2030. AI-assisted diagnostics augmenting rather than replacing physicians.
sources: WHO Health Workforce 2030 — 10M global shortage projection
WHO Global Health Observatory: Physician density 2024

occupations.name: Nurses & Midwives
occupations.description: Largest healthcare workforce globally. Severe shortage: 5.9M nurse deficit (WHO 2023). Migration from Philippines, India, Africa to OECD nations.
sources: WHO State of the World's Nursing 2023 — 5.9M global nurse deficit
ICN (International Council of Nurses) Workforce Data 2024

occupations.name: Community & Allied Health Workers
occupations.description: Community health workers, paramedics, physiotherapists. Backbone of PHC in low-income nations; growing in ageing high-income countries.
sources: WHO Global Health Workforce Statistics 2024
ILO Care Economy Jobs Report 2023

occupations.name: Elderly & Social Care Workers
occupations.description: Fastest-growing healthcare segment due to global ageing. 1.4Bn people over 60 by 2030 (UN). Japan, Germany, South Korea facing acute shortages.
sources: ILO Care Work and Care Jobs for the Future 2022
UN World Population Ageing Report 2023 — 1.4Bn over 60 by 2030

sectors.name: Education
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Education 2.7% of global workforce
UNESCO Global Education Monitoring Report 2024
ILO Teachers and Education Personnel 2023

occupations.name: School Teachers (K-12)
occupations.description: UNESCO estimates 69M new teachers needed by 2030 to achieve SDG 4. Sub-Saharan Africa faces the most acute shortage.
sources: UNESCO Global Education Monitoring Report 2024 — 69M teacher shortage
ILO Teachers in the 21st Century 2023
UIS (UNESCO Institute for Statistics) Teacher Data 2024

occupations.name: University & Higher Education Faculty
occupations.description: 216M students enrolled globally (UNESCO 2024). Research universities under pressure to adopt AI in curriculum.
sources: UNESCO IESALC Higher Education Global Report 2022
ILO Academic Staff Working Conditions 2023

occupations.name: EdTech & Online Learning
occupations.description: Global EdTech market $350Bn by 2025 (HolonIQ). Coursera, Udemy, Duolingo and 20,000+ startups creating new instructional design roles.
sources: HolonIQ Global EdTech Market Intelligence 2024 — $350Bn projection
UNESCO IESALC Online Education 2024

sectors.name: IT & Digital Economy
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — ICT sector employment
ITU Global ICT Skills Outlook 2024
NASSCOM Global Technology Talent Report 2025
Stack Overflow Developer Survey 2024

occupations.name: Software Developers & Engineers
occupations.description: GitHub reports 100M+ developers globally. US, India, China dominate. Copilot-style AI raising productivity but not yet displacing jobs.
sources: GitHub Octoverse State of Open Source Report 2024 — 100M developers
Stack Overflow Developer Survey 2024 — salaries and demographics
OECD ICT Employment Indicators 2024

occupations.name: AI / ML Engineers & Researchers
occupations.description: Fastest-growing and highest-paid tech role globally. Only 300K people worldwide have deep ML expertise (Element AI 2019); gap widening with demand.
sources: LinkedIn AI Jobs on the Rise 2024 — AI roles grew 74% in 4 years
Stanford AI Index Report 2024
Global AI Talent Report 2023 — talent concentration analysis

occupations.name: Cybersecurity Professionals
occupations.description: 4M unfilled cybersecurity positions globally (ISC2 2024). Ransomware costing $8Tr annually (Cybersecurity Ventures). Demand structurally outpacing supply.
sources: ISC2 Cybersecurity Workforce Study 2024 — 4M global gap
Cybersecurity Ventures Cybercrime Report 2024 — $8Tr annual cost

occupations.name: Cloud & DevOps Engineers
occupations.description: AWS, Azure, GCP combined revenue $270Bn (FY2024). Cloud engineers among the most sought-after profiles in every country.
sources: Gartner Worldwide Public Cloud Services Forecast 2024 — $679Bn cloud market
LinkedIn Global Talent Trends 2024

occupations.name: Data Scientists & Analysts
occupations.description: Data roles in every industry vertical. World Economic Forum calls data analysts the #1 fastest-growing job category globally.
sources: WEF Future of Jobs Report 2025 — Data Analysts #1 growing role
LinkedIn Emerging Jobs Report 2024

occupations.name: IT Support & BPO
occupations.description: Highest AI-risk tech role. LLMs now handle Tier-1 support. Sector shifting to AI training, quality assurance, exception handling.
sources: Gartner Predicts 2024 — 80% of customer service interactions AI-handled by 2025
NASSCOM BPO Sector Global Competitive Analysis 2025

sectors.name: Finance & Insurance
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Finance sector employment
World Bank Financial Development Report 2024
BIS Annual Economic Report 2024

occupations.name: Bank Employees
occupations.description: Global banking employs 20M; headcount declining in developed markets as AI handles back-office. Jobs growing in emerging markets.
sources: BIS Annual Economic Report 2024 — banking sector employment trends
ILO Finance Sector Future of Work 2023

occupations.name: FinTech Professionals
occupations.description: Global FinTech investment $113Bn in 2023 (CB Insights). Neobanks, crypto, DeFi, embedded finance creating new role categories.
sources: CB Insights State of FinTech 2024 — $113Bn global investment
WEF Future of Financial Services 2024

occupations.name: Investment Advisors & Analysts
occupations.description: Wealth management, equity research, M&A advisory. AUM automation (robo-advisors like Betterment, Wealthfront) disrupting retail advisory.
sources: CFA Institute Global Employment Survey 2024
Bloomberg AI in Finance Special Report 2024

occupations.name: Insurance Agents & Underwriters
occupations.description: Global insurance premium $7Tr (Swiss Re 2024). Underwriting being automated by ML; agent roles shifting to advisory.
sources: Swiss Re World Insurance Report 2024 — $7Tr global premium
Accenture Future of Insurance Workforce 2024

occupations.name: Accountants & Auditors
occupations.description: Big 4 employing 1.5M globally. AI (CoCounsel, Harvey, ChatGPT-4) automating routine compliance and audit tasks at pace.
sources: IFAC (International Federation of Accountants) Global Accountancy Report 2024
Deloitte Future of Audit Report 2024

sectors.name: Government & Public Admin
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Public Admin 4.5% global workforce
UN DESA World Public Sector Report 2023
World Bank Government Effectiveness Index 2023

occupations.name: Civil Servants & Government Officers
occupations.description: Government bureaucracies in 195 countries. AI-driven e-government reducing routine paperwork roles; growing in digital governance.
sources: UN DESA Global e-Government Survey 2024
OECD Government at a Glance 2023 — public employment 18% of total employment in OECD

occupations.name: Police & Armed Forces
occupations.description: 27M active military + 28M police globally. Defence spending at post-Cold War highs ($2.24Tr, SIPRI 2023).
sources: SIPRI Military Expenditure Database 2024 — $2.24Tr global defence spending
UNODC World Drug Report 2024 — police workforce data

occupations.name: Social Workers & Welfare Staff
occupations.description: Growing demand from ageing populations, mental health crisis, refugee crises. IFSW estimates 3M professional social workers globally.
sources: IFSW (International Federation of Social Workers) Global Report 2023
OECD Health at a Glance 2023 — social care workforce

sectors.name: Professional Services
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Professional services 4.5% global workforce
IBISWorld Global Professional Services Report 2024

occupations.name: Lawyers & Legal Professionals
occupations.description: Global legal services market $1.1Tr. AI tools (Harvey, CoCounsel) automating contract review, due diligence — disrupting paralegal and junior associate roles.
sources: Thomson Reuters Future of Professionals Report 2024
IBISWorld Global Legal Services Market 2024 — $1.1Tr

occupations.name: Management Consultants
occupations.description: McKinsey, BCG, Bain and Big 4 consulting employ 5M. AI reshaping deliverables; firms investing heavily in GenAI practices.
sources: Source Global Research Consulting Market Review 2024
MCA (Management Consultancies Association) Annual Report 2024

occupations.name: Marketing & Advertising
occupations.description: Global ad spend $1.1Tr (Dentsu 2025). Generative AI disrupting copywriting, image creation; growing demand for AI prompt engineers and data-driven marketers.
sources: Dentsu Global Ad Spend Forecast 2025 — $1.1Tr
WPP AI and Creativity Report 2024

occupations.name: Architects & Industrial Designers
occupations.description: AI generative design (Autodesk, Midjourney) changing workflows. Architects using AI to explore 1000x more design variants.
sources: UIA (Union Internationale des Architectes) Global Profession Report 2024
Autodesk State of Design & Make Report 2024

occupations.name: HR & Recruitment Professionals
occupations.description: AI screening tools (HireVue, Pymetrics, LinkedIn AI) handling 70% of resume screening. HR shifting to strategic people analytics.
sources: SHRM (Society for Human Resource Management) Workplace 2024 Survey
LinkedIn Future of Recruiting Report 2024

occupations.name: Scientists & R&D Researchers
occupations.description: 8.8M researchers globally (UNESCO 2024). AI dramatically accelerating drug discovery (AlphaFold), materials science, climate modelling.
sources: UNESCO Science Report 2023 — 8.8M researchers globally
Nature Index AI in Science Special Report 2024

occupations.name: Journalists & Media Professionals
occupations.description: Newsroom employment fell 26% in 2008-2020 (ILO). AI-generated news threatening further contraction; investigative journalism more valued.
sources: IFJ (International Federation of Journalists) Global Employment Report 2024
Reuters Institute Digital News Report 2024

occupations.name: Business Process & Admin Support
occupations.description: Data entry, admin assistants, bookkeepers. Highest AI disruption risk in white-collar work. WEF projects 26M such jobs displaced by 2027.
sources: WEF Future of Jobs Report 2025 — 26M admin jobs at risk
OECD Employment Outlook 2024 — automation risk by occupation

sectors.name: Mining & Energy
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Mining + Utilities sector employment
IEA World Energy Employment 2024
IRENA Renewable Energy and Jobs 2024

occupations.name: Oil, Gas & Coal Workers
occupations.description: IEA Net Zero Scenario requires no new O&G fields beyond 2021. Just transition concerns for 12M workers in fossil fuels.
sources: IEA World Energy Outlook 2024 — fossil fuel employment 12M
ILO Just Transition Guidelines 2024

occupations.name: Renewable Energy Workers
occupations.description: 16.2M renewable energy jobs globally in 2023 (IRENA) — solar PV 7.1M, wind 1.3M. Fastest growing energy employment category.
sources: IRENA Renewable Energy and Jobs Annual Review 2024 — 16.2M jobs
IEA World Energy Employment 2024

occupations.name: Mining & Quarrying Workers
occupations.description: Critical mineral mining (lithium, cobalt, rare earths) booming for EV batteries and electronics. Autonomous mining vehicles (Rio Tinto, BHP) automating haul trucks.
sources: IEA Critical Minerals Market Review 2024
Mining.com Global Mining Employment Statistics 2024

occupations.name: Power Grid & Utility Workers
occupations.description: Electricity grid modernisation for net-zero requires $21Tr in grid investment by 2050 (IEA). Smart grid technicians in high demand.
sources: IEA World Energy Investment 2024 — $21Tr grid investment needed
CEA/ENTSO-E Grid Employment Outlook 2024

sectors.name: Accommodation & Food Services
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Accommodation & Food 4.5% global workforce
UNWTO World Tourism Barometer 2024
World Travel & Tourism Council Economic Impact Report 2024

occupations.name: Chefs, Cooks & Restaurant Staff
occupations.description: Food service employs 72M globally. Post-COVID labour shortages in OECD nations driving wages up. Robot chefs remain limited to narrow tasks.
sources: ILO Hotels, Catering & Tourism Sector Report 2023
NRA (National Restaurant Association) Global Industry Report 2024

occupations.name: Hotel & Hospitality Workers
occupations.description: Tourism recovered to 88% of 2019 levels in 2023 (UNWTO). 1.5Bn international arrivals projected by 2030. AI concierge and self-check-in growing.
sources: UNWTO World Tourism Barometer April 2024
WTTC Economic Impact Report 2024 — 330M travel & tourism jobs

occupations.name: Tour Guides & Travel Agents
occupations.description: Travel agents declining (AI booking), but tour guides and experience designers growing with experiential travel trend.
sources: UNWTO Tourism Statistics 2024
WTTC Future of Travel Report 2024

sectors.name: Personal & Domestic Services
sources: ILO ILOSTAT 2023 — Personal Services 3.3% global workforce
ILO Domestic Workers Global Report 2023
ILO Women and Men in the Informal Economy 2023

occupations.name: Domestic Workers
occupations.description: ILO estimates 75.6M domestic workers globally; 76% women, majority informal. Philippines, Indonesia, Ethiopia major source countries.
sources: ILO Making Decent Work a Reality for Domestic Workers 2023 — 75.6M workers
ILO Women and Men in the Informal Economy 2023

occupations.name: Beauty, Wellness & Fitness
occupations.description: Global wellness economy $5.6Tr (GWI 2023). Fastest growth in Asia-Pacific personal care and fitness segments.
sources: Global Wellness Institute Economy Report 2024 — $5.6Tr market
IBISWorld Global Salons & Beauty Services 2024

occupations.name: Security Guards & Services
occupations.description: Global private security market $350Bn. AI surveillance (facial recognition, anomaly detection) augmenting guards, not yet replacing.
sources: Freedonia Global Security Services Market Report 2024 — $350Bn
CoESS (Confederation of European Security Services) World Report 2024
